additions.template: just got off the phone with {personName:entity}
when did i last catch up with {personName:contact}
when did i last hear from {personName:contact}
when was the last time i talked to {personName:contact}
when was the last time i spoke to {personName:contact}
when was the last time i saw {personName:contact}
when's the last time i talked to {personName:contact}
when did i talk to {personName:contact} last
how long has it been since i saw {personName:contact}
how long since i caught up with {personName:contact}
what interactions have i had with {personName:contact}
what have i told you about {personName:contact}
what did i tell you about {personName:contact}
what notes do i have on {personName:contact}
tell me what i know about {personName:contact}
who has a birthday coming up
who's got a birthday coming up
are there any birthdays coming up
do i have any birthdays coming up
do you know when {personName:contact}'s birthday is

gaps.want: spoke to X yesterday / had lunch with X on Friday (dated interaction capture)
gaps.needs: log-interaction skill only stamps compute:today; needs an optional {at:date} input plus templates like 'talked to {personName:entity} on {at:date}' and 'spoke to {personName:entity} yesterday'

gaps.want: who do i know that likes hiking (reverse fact search)
gaps.needs: new skill: scan contact_fact across all contacts for a query substring and group matches by subject; no read op today spans facts without a fixed subject

gaps.want: how are Anna and Tom related
gaps.needs: two-person relationship query; list-relations takes a single personName — needs a new skill or a second optional {otherName:contact} input with filtered output

gaps.want: my sister Emma loves pottery (self-relative capture)
gaps.needs: a first-person/owner contact concept so relationTo can resolve to 'me'; remember-person-fact currently requires a named relationTo entity

gaps.want: how old is X / when does X turn 40
gaps.needs: age computation from contact.birthday (years-since / next-age function in DSL); when-birthday only reports next occurrence and days-until

gaps.want: Sarah is allergic to peanuts (anchored predicate capture without 'remember')
gaps.needs: DSL support to prepend the matched literal anchor into the stored slot value; today a template like '{p} is allergic to {fact}' would store only 'peanuts', losing the predicate. Bare '{p:entity} is {fact:text}' is untemplatable — it would hijack world statements like 'Paris is France's capital'

gaps.want: who did i talk to this week / who haven't i talked to in a while (neglected contacts)
gaps.needs: new skill iterating all contacts' interactions with a date-range filter or per-contact max(at) comparison

gaps.want: remove X's nickname / stop calling X Y
gaps.needs: set-alias only appends to the aliases csv; a remove-alias skill (string removal from the field) does not exist

gaps.want: when is X's anniversary / other per-person dates
gaps.needs: contact type only has a birthday field; arbitrary named dates would need a contact_date record type and skills

notes: SLOT-TYPE POLICY: all new person-QUERY templates use {personName:contact} so unknown-name questions ('who is the president', 'what is the capital of France', 'tell me about the roman empire') cannot be hijacked — they fall through to cloud. Capture templates (remember-person-fact, log-interaction, set-birthday, forget-fact, remember-relationship) keep {personName:entity} because the person may be new, and every one carries a literal anchor (remember/note/jot/talked to/had lunch with/etc.). Exception: new set-alias templates use :contact because the skill itself refuses unknown contacts ('I don't have X as a contact yet'), so :contact is strictly safer with no behavior loss. MIGRATION SUGGESTION: existing corpus query templates for last-interaction, list-interactions, list-relations, recall-facts (lines 41-59, 69-70, 127-128 of corpus.json) still use :entity — 'tell me about {personName:entity}' and 'how is {personName:entity} doing' are the worst over-matchers and should be migrated to :contact. ORDERING/COLLISIONS to verify in the router: (1) when-birthday "what is {p:contact}'s birthday" vs recall-fact-about "what is {p:contact} {query:text}" and "what is {p:contact}'s {query:text}" — the birthday literal must outrank the generic query template (more literal tokens should win); same for "is {p}'s birthday coming up" vs "is {p} {query}". (2) remember-relationship "remember that X is Y's Z" overlaps remember-person-fact "remember that X {fact}" (fact='is Y's Z') — the relationship template has more resolved slots and must rank higher so it writes contact_relationship instead of a free-text fact. (3) set-alias "{p} goes by the name {alias}" must outrank existing "{p} goes by {alias}" or the alias gets stored as 'the name X'. (4) log-interaction "called {p:entity}" (past tense, existing) vs any future phone-command intents — kept new imperative-looking 'call {p} {alias}' OUT of set-alias for this reason. (5) recall-facts vs recall-fact-about split: bare 'what do i know about X' = all facts; trailing-predicate forms = filtered substring search; 'who is {p:contact}' (recall-facts) cannot collide with 'who is {p:contact}'s {query}' (recall-fact-about) since the possessive+query requires extra tokens. (6) 'any birthdays this month' maps to the 30-day window of upcoming-birthdays — approximate but acceptable offline. 'texted/facetimed {p:entity}' are single-token anchors on an entity slot; if the entity matcher is loose, consider tightening these to :contact.
